mnemonics: HOT POT (Hold One Thing, Partially Observe The other) - For partial differentiation, hold all variables constant except the one you are differentiating with respect to.
keyPoints: A partial derivative measures the rate of change of a function with respect to one variable while holding all other variables constant.
The gradient (∇f) is a vector containing all the first-order partial derivatives of a function, pointing in the direction of the steepest ascent.
The chain rule for partial derivatives (e.g., ∂z/∂x = (∂z/∂u)(∂u/∂x) + (∂z/∂v)(∂v/∂x)) is essential for differentiating composite functions.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to treat other variables as constants when computing a partial derivative with respect to a specific variable.
Incorrectly applying the single-variable chain rule instead of the multi-variable chain rule for partial derivatives.
Confusing the notation for partial derivatives (∂) with the notation for ordinary derivatives (d).
